MOODBOARD · 001
ISMILE × IMVI
APRIL 2026
Brand direction — for internal review

The stage
is set in
gold.

A visual direction for IMVI — where the brand becomes the theatre that every young athlete gets to stand on. Restrained on the outside. Personal on the inside. Premium at every touchpoint.

Direction A · Editorial Sports Broadcast
›››››
Page 01 / 07
01 / The vision

Two layers,
one system.

IMVI's visual language operates on two altitudes. The brand speaks in restraint and gold. The athletes on the platform speak in color, attitude, and their club's own voice. They never compete — they complement.

Layer 01 · The stage

Broadcast-grade. Quiet. Premium.

The app chrome, marketing surfaces, admin console, and IMVI's own voice. Black canvas, brushed gold as hero material, editorial typography. This is the theatre. It stays out of the way and lets the moment matter.

Y-3 New Balance Jordan Mi Casa ESPYs broadcast
Layer 02 · The athletes

Loud. Personal. Theirs.

The cards themselves. Each one carries its club's colors, textures, and swagger — splatter, smoke, brush, whatever the team's identity demands. The template is IMVI's. The voice is the athlete's.

PFA splatter NxLVL smoke Batrs grit Club-owned
The connection

The stage hosts the athletes. The athletes fill the stage. When a kid scans their card, the loud personal moment plays back on the quiet premium surface. That's the whole product in one sentence.

02 / References

Where IMVI lives
on the map.

A grounding in the visual neighborhood Kevin has consistently pointed to. These are not aspirations we're copying — they're the altitude we're designing at.

01 · Architectural athleticwear
Y-3 / Yohji
Black canvas, asymmetry earned, Japanese restraint. The tone of the IMVI stage.
02
Mi Casa
Jordan Brand. Cultural weight, heritage type, lived-in luxury.
03
New Balance
Quiet-luxury, technical typography, near-monochrome restraint.
04 · Premium-athletic
Anta
Sharp modern athletic design. Gold that means something.
05 · Media-house energy
ESPN Broadcast
Data overlays, tickers, live-TV chrome — informing the admin console & app chrome.
06 · Drop culture
SNKRS
Countdown energy, limited-edition framing for highlight drops & IMVI awards.
03 / Palette

Four materials.
No decoration.

The palette is deliberately small. Ink is the canvas. Gold is the hero material. Bone is the voice. Red is for live only. Everything the product does happens inside this range.

01 Canvas
Obsidian
#0A0A0A
02 Hero material
Brushed gold
Gradient · 5C–F0
03 Voice
Bone
#F5F1E8
04 Utility only
Live red
#E53935
On obsidian
Not pure black. A deep ink with undertone, so gold has somewhere to catch light. The current IMVI.me site already commits to this canvas — we keep it and evolve.
On brushed gold
Rendered as a gradient material, not a flat fill. It catches light, reads metallic, and is reserved for premium moments: the logo, hero display type, "IMVI Awards" surfaces, card frames in the house state.
On live red
Demoted from brand color to utility indicator. Used only for: live-streaming status, alert banners, delete actions, rejection states. Never for decorative emphasis.
04 / Typography

Two voices.
One system.

A display face that carries both the editorial weight and the broadcast-data moments. A workhorse that handles every piece of body UI. Two faces, two jobs — no third voice muddying the signal.

Display + Data · Oswald / Druk*
Athlete
of the
year.
For names, headlines, stats, timestamps, scan IDs Weight 500 / 700

A card is scanned. A moment plays back. Every view is a vote. Every vote is a step toward a year-end ceremony where a club's name is called and a kid walks on stage.This is the full IMVI system in sixty words.
For UI, descriptions, body copy Weight 300/400/500

* Display + data rendered in Oswald for this board and for the POC build (web-safe, self-hosted WOFF2). Production recommendation: Druk Wide or Monument Extended — both on Kevin’s font list and both carry the editorial-sports gravity the brand needs. Per spec §2.1, the earlier Orbitron "data face" was dropped in v1.1: Oswald 700 handles large numbers, Oswald 500 uppercase with wide letter-spacing handles small labels and chyrons.

05 / Card system

Structure is ours.
Style is theirs.

The card template is a structural scaffold — slots for logo, number, photo, name, wordmark, chevrons. Every slot’s visual fill is owned by the club. Their colors. Their textures. Their energy. IMVI never prescribes the style, only the structure.

Principle 01
The template
owns the slots.
Position of logo, jersey number, photo, name lockup, IMVI wordmark, chevron decoration, and frame. Consistent across every team. These are the IMVI fingerprints that thread the system together.
Principle 02
The club
owns the skin.
Background color, texture style (splatter · smoke · brush · grit), typography tone, team logo, signature treatment. Each club brings its own voice into the template.
Two templates · drawn from the existing design language
Template A
— Action.
Athlete mid-motion. Cutout photography dominates. Team logo top-center, jersey number offset, script signature over heavy block last name. The format that reads loudest from across a room.
Slot map
  • Team logoClub asset
  • Jersey numberRegistration
  • Athlete cutoutParent upload
  • Background + textureClub skin
  • Name lockup (script + block)Registration
  • IMVI wordmark + chevronsSystem
Template B
— Portrait.
Athlete standing, composed, confident. Atmospheric backgrounds instead of splatter. Team logo upper-left, jersey upper-right, torn color band behind the name. A quieter sibling, same system.
Slot map
  • Team logo + cityClub asset
  • Jersey numberRegistration
  • Athlete portraitParent upload
  • Atmospheric backgroundClub skin
  • Name lockup (script + block)Registration
  • IMVI wordmark + chevronsSystem
The same system · three club skins
One scaffold. Infinite voices.
Each club brings a color palette, a texture signature, a typography tone, and a logo. The template renders those inputs into a consistent structural layout. Below: three real IMVI cards, showing how the system accommodates wildly different club identities without losing its core fingerprint — the frame, the chevrons, the IMVI wordmark anchor.
PFA skin · Yellow · Splatter
NxLVL skin · Purple · Smoke
Batrs skin · Navy + yellow · Grit
Every card has a back · team-owned territory
Front is the athlete. Back is the club.
The back of the card is where the club’s identity lives — mascot, city, colors, atmosphere. In the IMVI platform, the back is also where sponsor content and highlight video previews surface. The template preserves the structural marks (frame, chevrons, IMVI wordmark) while everything else is the club’s.
PFA back · Team-branded
NxLVL back · Mascot-led
Batrs back · Script-anchored
The anchor · IMVI house card

When IMVI speaks for itself,
it speaks in gold.

The house card uses the same structural template — same frame, same wordmark, same chevrons — rendered in IMVI’s own brand language instead of a club’s. It appears in empty states, sponsor placeholders, onboarding tutorials, and anywhere the platform represents itself.

It’s the proof that the template and the brand are the same system — just with different skins applied.

06 / Motion & material

Considered.
Never decorative.

Every transition earns its place. The Japanese-function principle — balance, inner strength — translates to motion that's confident, unhurried, and never performs for its own sake.

M01
Ease, never bounce.
Cubic-bezier curves that slow into their destination. Nothing oscillates. Nothing overshoots. The brand is too premium for springs.
M02
Gold catches light.
On hover, tap, or reveal, gold surfaces get a single specular sweep — left-to-right, subtle, 800ms. Never a glow. Never a pulse. Light moves across material.
M03
Cards have weight.
Flipping front-to-back uses 3D rotation with a slight delay on the content crossfade — so the physics read first, then the information. The card is an object.
M04
Stage lighting reveals.
Hero moments (AR card unlock, awards screens, player intros) fade in from black through the gold floor-fade — the same atmospheric treatment as the marketing site, but animated.
07 / The hero moment

Scan the card.
Unlock the
moment.

The AR scan is IMVI's first impression in every demo, every install, every living room. It has to feel like walking out from the tunnel. The viewfinder is simple. The reward is the stage lighting up underneath the card as the moment plays back.

SCAN AR READY
